question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data was officially released by the BLS on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase, which is well below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. Despite geopolitical tensions with Iran causing energy price spikes, the actual CPI reading was significantly lower than needed. The market should resolve to NO with certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Iran conflict caused energy price spikes but insufficient to reach 0.8% threshold
Market closes April 10, same day as data release
Clear resolution criteria with single-decimal precision
risks: Data revision possibility (though rare for such large changes)
Alternative data sources showing higher readings
